title: Whole Exome and RNA Sequencing Analyses of PDAC Samples
author: Do Young Hyeon
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.21203/rs.3.pex-2062/v1
materials: SureSelect Human All Exon v5 probe set

steps.title: To remove the discrepancies in tissues...
steps.instruction: To remove the discrepancies in tissues acquired for genomic, proteomic, and pathological analyses, we adopted a new tissue processing strategy. Briefly, the tumour tissues were cryo-pulverised to obtain a fine tissue powder. The tissue powder was then divided into aliquots for DNA and RNA (ca. 3 mg) isolation and protein extraction (15 mg). This strategy allowed us to produce all proteogenomic data (e.g. WES, mRNAseq, global proteome, and phosphoproteome) and acquire tumour cellularity data from an equivalent tissue sample.

steps.title: DNA and RNA were extracted according...
steps.instruction: DNA and RNA were extracted according to the Allspin (GeneAll) total DNA/RNA purification protocol. Briefly, the tissue powder was immersed in RNAlater reagent (R0901, Sigma-Aldrich) immediately after homogenization. Buffer CTL (+β-mercaptoethanol; 350 µL) and tungsten carbide beads (3 mm) were added to the sample tubes; the tissue powder was further lysed by Tissuelyzer at ˗25 Hz for 3 min. The lysate was incubated for 10 min at room temperature, and the lysate in the spin column was centrifuged at 10,000 g for 3 min to separate the DNA and RNA. The pass-through was used for RNA extraction, and the remaining lysate in the mini spin column was used for DNA extraction. The concentration and integrity were measured using a bioanalyzer (Agilent) before library construction.

steps.title: with the MEM algorithm 1
steps.instruction: with the MEM algorithm 1 . Using Samtools (version 1.4.

steps.title: 2 , SAM files were further...
steps.instruction: 2 , SAM files were further processed to generate, sort, and index BAM files. After alignment, duplicate reads were removed using Picard (version 2.18.
3 . IndelRealigner and BaseRecalibrator modules of the Genome Analysis Tool Kit (GATK; version 3.

steps.title: 4 were used for pre-processing before variant calling
steps.instruction: 4 were used for pre-processing before variant calling. Germline variants in blood samples were identified by HaplotypeCaller, and somatic variants were called using Mutect2 (version 3.

steps.title: 5 from the tumours, when compared...
steps.instruction: 5 from the tumours, when compared with matched blood samples, according to the GATK best practice.
6 . The exonic variants were annotated using ANNOVAR (version 2018Apr

steps.title: 7 , and annovar tables were...
steps.instruction: 7 , and annovar tables were converted to MAF files and visualized using maftools 8 R package. Coverage and covariate files provided by MutSigCV were used to calculate q values. Mutated genes with a q value < 0.05 were defined as the SMGs. When determining SMGs, biallelic somatic single nucleotide variants (SNVs) were not considered. For CNV analysis, CNVkit (version 0.9.

steps.title: 9 was applied to the WES...
steps.instruction: 9 was applied to the WES data to obtain segmented copy data. Segmented data that did not correspond with the contigs of 22 major autosomes and sex chromosomes were filtered out. The filtered data were analysed using GISTIC2.0 (version 2.0.

steps.title: 10 to identify copy number-altered genomic regions and genes
steps.instruction: 10 to identify copy number-altered genomic regions and genes. These variants were categorised into missense and nonsense mutations, in-frame insertions, in-frame deletions, frameshift insertions, and frameshift deletions according to the snpEff (version 4.3t) 11 annotation. Exome coverage was defined as shared genomic regions between capture probe regions and callable regions using the GATK CallableLoic tool. Tumour mutation burden was defined as the number of protein sequence-altering variants divided by the exome coverage.

steps.title: To obtain high-quality data and overcome...
steps.instruction: To obtain high-quality data and overcome RNA degradation problems in the PDAC tissues, exome capture RNA sequencing was performed. After quantifying total RNA using Quant-IT RiboGreen, the samples were run on the TapeStation RNA ScreenTape to calculate DV200, a percentage of RNA fragments of over 200 nucleotides. The mRNA encoding exome was isolated from >100 ng of total RNA using the Illumina TruSeq RNA exome according to the manufacturer’s instructions. The strand-specific cDNA library was constructed with adaptor ligation and then sequenced with paired-end 101 bp reads using the Illumina HiSeq 2500 platform. Adaptors and reads of low quality or short length were trimmed using Trimmomatic (version 0.36) 12 . The trimmed reads were aligned to the GRCh38 genome using STAR (version 2.5.3a) 13 . The resulting mapped reads were used to quantify the expression levels for genes according to GENCODE v27 GTF annotation using RSEM (version 1.3.0) 14 .

steps.title: Proteogenomic landscape of human pancreatic ductal...
steps.instruction: Proteogenomic landscape of human pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma in an Asian population reveals tumor cell-enriched and immune-rich subtypes by Do Young Hyeon, Dowoon Nam, Youngmin Han, Duk Ki Kim, Gibeom Kim, Daeun Kim, Jingi Bae, Seunghoon Back, Dong-Gi Mun, Inamul Hasan Madar, Hangyeore Lee, Su-Jin Kim, Hokeun Kim, Sangyeop Hyun, Chang Rok Kim, Seon Ah Choi, Yong Ryoul Kim, Juhee Jeong, Suwan Jeon, Yeon Woong Choo, Kyung Bun Lee, Wooil Kwon, Seunghyuk Choi, Taewan Goo, Taesung Park, Young-Ah Suh, Hongbeom Kim, Ja-Lok Ku, Min-Sik Kim, Eunok Paek, Daechan Park, Keehoon Jung, Sung Hee Baek, Jin-Young Jang, Daehee Hwang, Sang-Won Lee published in Nature Cancer on 22 Dec, 2022 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/s43018-022-00479-7
